title: Extreme heat will kill millions of people in Europe without rapid action
description: Climate models predict that the number of heat-related deaths could soar in cities over the coming century, even when efforts are made to keep people safe.  Climate models predict that the number of heat-related deaths could soar in cities over the coming century, even when efforts are made to keep people safe.
bodyText:
Access to this article via DEAL Open Access Services (DEAS) gGmbH is not available. An extra 2.3 million people in European cities could die as a result of extreme temperatures — both hot and cold — by the end of the century if countries do not take action to mitigate climate change, according to a study that modelled the effects of rising temperatures.
